expectations.text: EntityAccessControlHandler class exists for Project or Task that extends EntityAccessControlHandler and implements checkAccess() with a switch or match on $operation for at least 'view', 'update', and 'delete' operations (NOT just relying on the admin_permission annotation key -- the entity MUST reference a custom access handler class in its handlers array)
expectations.evidence: src/Access/ProjectAccessControlHandler.php: class extends EntityAccessControlHandler, implements checkAccess() with switch on $operation covering 'view' (line 25), 'update' (line 28), 'delete' (line 35). Entity annotation in src/Entity/Project.php line 21: "access" = "Drupal\group_ai_pm\Access\ProjectAccessControlHandler"

expectations.text: At least one AccessResult in an access handler uses ->addCacheContexts() or ->addCacheTags() (bare AccessResult::allowed() or ::forbidden() without cache metadata causes the first user's access decision to be cached and applied to ALL subsequent users -- AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() is also acceptable as it adds cache contexts automatically)
expectations.evidence: src/Access/ProjectAccessControlHandler.php lines 26, 31, 33, 39-40: uses AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() throughout checkAccess(), which automatically adds cache contexts. Same pattern in TaskAccessControlHandler.php.

expectations.text: permissions.yml uses `permission_callbacks` key pointing to a PHP class method that generates per-entity CRUD permissions dynamically (NOT hardcoding all 12+ permissions as static YAML entries -- permission_callbacks is the Drupal pattern for generating permissions programmatically from entity types or bundles)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.permissions.yml lines 10-12: permission_callbacks pointing to Drupal\group_ai_pm\PermissionCallbacks::projectPermissions and ::taskPermissions, which dynamically generate CRUD permissions via loops in src/PermissionCallbacks.php.

expectations.text: At least one plugin class (Block, AiFunctionCall, or other) implements ContainerFactoryPluginInterface AND has a create() method with the 4-parameter signature: create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) -- NOT the controller 1-parameter signature create(ContainerInterface $container) which causes a fatal error when the plugin manager passes 4 arguments
expectations.evidence: src/Plugin/Block/ProjectStatusBlock.php line 55: public static function create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition). Same 4-param signature in CreateProjectTool.php line 53 and QueryProjectsTool.php line 53. All implement ContainerFactoryPluginInterface.

expectations.text: At least one plugin constructor calls parent::__construct($configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) before storing injected services (without this call, the plugin loses its configuration array, plugin ID, and definition metadata -- block placement settings and plugin inspection methods break silently)
expectations.evidence: src/Plugin/Block/ProjectStatusBlock.php line 48: parent::__construct($configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition); before storing $entity_type_manager. Same in CreateProjectTool.php line 46 and QueryProjectsTool.php line 46.

expectations.text: Block plugin has a blockAccess() method that returns an AccessResult (restricting block visibility based on permissions -- without blockAccess(), the block is visible to everyone including anonymous users regardless of what content it displays)
expectations.evidence: src/Plugin/Block/ProjectStatusBlock.php lines 94-97: protected function blockAccess(AccountInterface $account) { return AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission($account, 'access group_ai_pm dashboard'); }

expectations.text: New admin/dangerous permissions (like 'delete any project' or 'administer group_ai_pm') use `restrict access: true` in permissions.yml or the permission_callbacks return array (this flag shows a security warning on the admin permissions page -- without it, site builders may grant dangerous permissions without realizing the risk)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.permissions.yml line 4 shows restrict access: true only for 'administer group_ai_pm'. However, src/PermissionCallbacks.php generates 'delete any project' and 'delete any task' permissions without restrict access: true in the returned array. The dangerous per-entity delete-any permissions lack this flag.

expectations.text: A route for the state-changing action (mark complete or similar) includes `_csrf_token: 'TRUE'` in its requirements section (non-form action links that change state MUST have CSRF protection -- without it, an attacker can craft a URL that changes entity state when clicked by an authenticated user)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml shows group_ai_pm.project_complete route at /admin/content/project/{project}/complete with requirements: _entity_access: 'project.update' only. No _csrf_token: 'TRUE' requirement is present. Runtime check 16 returned no output confirming absence.

expectations.text: NO static \Drupal:: service calls in any NEW plugin class files under src/Plugin/ (plugin classes that need services MUST inject them via ContainerFactoryPluginInterface create() + constructor -- \Drupal:: calls bypass DI, making plugins untestable)
expectations.evidence: grep for \Drupal:: in src/Plugin/ returned no matches. All three plugin files (ProjectStatusBlock.php, CreateProjectTool.php, QueryProjectsTool.php) inject services via create() and constructor only.

expectations.text: Entity annotation/attribute handlers array for at least one entity includes an 'access' key pointing to the custom access handler class (NOT just using admin_permission -- admin_permission only provides a global on/off check; custom access handlers enable per-operation, per-user, ownership-based access control)
expectations.evidence: src/Entity/Project.php lines 18-31: handlers annotation includes "access" = "Drupal\group_ai_pm\Access\ProjectAccessControlHandler". TaskAccessControlHandler is similarly referenced in src/Entity/Task.php.

expectations.text: (runtime) Module re-enables cleanly after code changes: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-without && ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:uninstall group_ai_pm -y && ddev drush en group_ai_pm -y` — should exit 0 with no fatal errors.
expectations.evidence: Command output: [success] Cache rebuild complete. [success] Successfully uninstalled: group_ai_pm. [success] Module group_ai_pm has been installed. (Permissions - Configure). Exit 0.

expectations.text: (runtime) Entity access handler is registered and instantiable: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-without && ddev drush php-eval "\$def = \Drupal::entityTypeManager()->getDefinition('project'); \$handler = \$def->getHandlerClass('access'); echo \$handler ? 'ACCESS_HANDLER:' . \$handler : 'NO_ACCESS_HANDLER';"` — output should contain "ACCESS_HANDLER:Drupal\group_ai_pm\"

(runtime) Dynamic permissions are generated and loadable: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-without && ddev drush php-eval "\$perms = \Drupal::service('user.permissions')->getPermissions(); \$found = array_filter(array_keys(\$perms), fn(\$p) => str_contains(\$p, 'project') && str_contains(\$p, 'own')); echo count(\$found) > 0 ? 'DYNAMIC_PERMS:' . implode(',', \$found) : 'NO_DYNAMIC_PERMS';"` — output should contain "DYNAMIC_PERMS:"
expectations.evidence: Command output: DYNAMIC_PERMS:delete own project,edit own project

expectations.text: (runtime) Block plugin is discoverable by plugin manager: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-without && ddev drush php-eval "\$blocks = \Drupal::service('plugin.manager.block')->getDefinitions(); \$found = array_filter(array_keys(\$blocks), fn(\$id) => str_contains(\$id, 'project_status') || str_contains(\$id, 'group_ai_pm')); echo count(\$found) > 0 ? 'BLOCK_FOUND:' . implode(',', \$found) : 'NO_BLOCK';"` — output should contain "BLOCK_FOUND:"

(runtime) Project entity CRUD works end-to-end: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-without && ddev drush php-eval "\$storage = \Drupal::entityTypeManager()->getStorage('project'); \$entity = \$storage->create(['title' => 'Runtime Test', 'status' => 'active']); \$entity->save(); \$loaded = \$storage->load(\$entity->id()); echo \$loaded ? 'CRUD_OK:' . \$loaded->get('title')->value : 'CRUD_FAIL';"` — output should contain "CRUD_OK:Runtime Test"
expectations.evidence: Command exited with code 1. Error: TypeError: call_user_func(): Argument #1 ($callback) must be a valid callback, class "Drupal\system\Entity\User" not found. Root cause: src/Entity/Project.php line 182 uses ->setDefaultValueCallback('Drupal\system\Entity\User::getCurrentUserId') which references a non-existent class path. The correct class is Drupal\user\Entity\User or \Drupal\Core\Session\AccountProxyInterface.

expectations.text: (runtime) CSRF-protected route exists in route collection: Run `cd /tmp/d10-phase15-without && ddev drush php-eval "\$routes = \Drupal::service('router.route_provider')->getRoutesByPattern('/admin/content/project/'); foreach (\$routes as \$name => \$route) { if (str_contains(\$name, 'complete')) { echo 'ROUTE_OK:csrf=' . (\$route->getRequirement('_csrf_token') ?? 'none'); } }"` — output should contain "ROUTE_OK:csrf=TRUE"
expectations.evidence: Command returned empty output. The group_ai_pm.project_complete route in routing.yml has no _csrf_token requirement. The route pattern searched ('/admin/content/project/') also may not match the actual route path ('/admin/content/project/{project}/complete'), but the primary issue is confirmed: no _csrf_token: 'TRUE' in routing.yml requirements.
